### Post by q.dinar on 2009-02-09
hello.
i want to open ports for transmission.
my iptables are currently these:
```
# Generated by iptables-save v1.4.0 on Mon Feb  9 19:22:43 2009
*nat
:PREROUTING ACCEPT [982560:347975559]
:POSTROUTING ACCEPT [3990:244709]
:OUTPUT ACCEPT [3997:245330]
COMMIT

:FORWARD ACCEPT [0:0]
:OUTPUT ACCEPT [148036:92903385]
:POSTROUTING ACCEPT [148016:92901594]
-A FORWARD -o ppp0 -p tcp -m tcp --tcp-flags SYN,RST SYN -m tcpmss --mss 1400:1536 -j TCPMSS --clamp-mss-to-pmtu 
-A FORWARD -o ppp0 -p tcp -m tcp --tcp-flags SYN,RST SYN -m tcpmss --mss 1400:1536 -j TCPMSS --clamp-mss-to-pmtu 
COMMIT

*filter
:INPUT ACCEPT [0:0]
:FORWARD ACCEPT [0:0]
:OUTPUT ACCEPT [0:0]
-A INPUT -i lo -j ACCEPT 
-A INPUT -m state --state RELATED,ESTABLISHED -j ACCEPT 
-A INPUT -p tcp -m tcp --dport 6667 -j ACCEPT 
-A INPUT -p tcp -m tcp --dport 80 -j ACCEPT 
-A INPUT -p udp -m udp --dport 123 -j ACCEPT 
-A INPUT -p tcp -m tcp --dport 8000 -j ACCEPT

-A FORWARD -j DROP 
-A OUTPUT -o lo -j ACCEPT 
-A OUTPUT -p tcp -m tcp --sport 80 -j ACCEPT 
-A OUTPUT -p tcp -m tcp --dport 8001 -j ACCEPT 
-A OUTPUT -p tcp -m tcp --sport 6667 -j ACCEPT 
-A OUTPUT -p tcp -m tcp --dport 6667 -j ACCEPT 
-A OUTPUT -p tcp -m tcp --dport 5190 -j ACCEPT 
-A OUTPUT -p udp -m udp --dport 53 -j ACCEPT 
-A OUTPUT -p tcp -m tcp --dport 443 -j ACCEPT 
-A OUTPUT -p tcp -m tcp --dport 80 -j ACCEPT 
-A OUTPUT -p udp -m udp --sport 123 -j ACCEPT 
-A OUTPUT -p tcp -m tcp --dport 25 -j ACCEPT 
-A OUTPUT -p tcp -m tcp --sport 8000 -j ACCEPT 
-A OUTPUT -j DROP 
COMMIT
# Completed on Mon Feb  9 19:22:43 2009
```
i want to open just needed ports for transmission. how to do that? once i tried to set to ACCEPT the port that is in transmission's configuration, it even has said that port is open, but did not work. as i remember even when i flushed INPUT and OUTPUT tables it did not work. then when i flushed FORWARD and stopped and started downloading it started to work, but i had installed ktorrent and was trying with it that time, (i did not need to install it... :( ). then i downloaded with flushed tables. but now i want to configure iptables. may be i just needed to stop and start downloading.

### Post by q.dinar on 2009-02-12
solved. i opened all outgoing connection and opened incoming with destination port 51413, udp and tcp protocols. this is that iptables:
```
# Generated by iptables-save v1.4.0 on Tue Feb 10 22:58:32 2009
*nat
:PREROUTING ACCEPT [2879406:922551687]
:POSTROUTING ACCEPT [1870:115920]
:OUTPUT ACCEPT [2007:124551]
COMMIT
# Completed on Tue Feb 10 22:58:32 2009
# Generated by iptables-save v1.4.0 on Tue Feb 10 22:58:32 2009
*mangle
:PREROUTING ACCEPT [3138365:1145924453]
:INPUT ACCEPT [1376246:600909515]
:FORWARD ACCEPT [0:0]
:OUTPUT ACCEPT [257535:195462448]
:POSTROUTING ACCEPT [257382:195452377]
-A FORWARD -o ppp0 -p tcp -m tcp --tcp-flags SYN,RST SYN -m tcpmss --mss 1400:1536 -j TCPMSS --clamp-mss-to-pmtu 
-A FORWARD -o ppp0 -p tcp -m tcp --tcp-flags SYN,RST SYN -m tcpmss --mss 1400:1536 -j TCPMSS --clamp-mss-to-pmtu 
COMMIT
# Completed on Tue Feb 10 22:58:32 2009
# Generated by iptables-save v1.4.0 on Tue Feb 10 22:58:32 2009
*filter
:INPUT ACCEPT [0:0]
:FORWARD ACCEPT [0:0]
:OUTPUT ACCEPT [30563:3054162]
-A INPUT -i lo -j ACCEPT 
-A INPUT -m state --state RELATED,ESTABLISHED -j ACCEPT 
-A INPUT -p tcp -m tcp --dport 6667 -j ACCEPT 
-A INPUT -p tcp -m tcp --dport 80 -j ACCEPT 
-A INPUT -p udp -m udp --dport 123 -j ACCEPT 
-A INPUT -p tcp -m tcp --dport 8000 -j ACCEPT 
-A INPUT -p udp -m udp --dport 51413 -j ACCEPT 
-A INPUT -p tcp -m tcp --dport 51413 -j ACCEPT 
-A INPUT -j DROP 
-A FORWARD -j DROP 
COMMIT
# Completed on Tue Feb 10 22:58:32 2009
```
51413 is port that is set in transmission's configuration.
i do not use this iptables when i switch transmission off, i do sudo iptables-restore < /path/to/oldiptables.txt .

---

### Post by q.dinar on 2009-02-12
i tell how i configure this. i do not edit it with text editor.
i write in terminal sudo iptables-save and see current iptables. then i have written sudo iptables -F OUTPUT and then again sudo iptables-save to see that they are flushed. then i am going to insert new rule before the last "DROP" rule of "INPUT" part (INPUT chain) of iptables. for that i count rules from the first rule of "input" starting from 1. number of "drop" rule is 7. then i write this: sudo iptables -I INPUT 7 -p tcp --dport 51413 -j ACCEPT . then i press "up" key to get this last command back and edit it to "sudo iptables -I INPUT 7 -p udp --dport 51413 -j ACCEPT" and press "enter". also when i wrote "sudo iptables-save" second time i pressed "up" key two times. then, now i again run "sudo iptables-save" command to see the resulting rules. then i saved the rules to a file: sudo iptables-save > /path/to/iptables-transmissionbt.txt .

---
